title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI named a Leader in enterprise coding agents by Gartner
summary: OpenAI leads in enterprise AI coding agents, AdventHealth uses ChatGPT for Healthcare

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. I've got some interesting updates for you today, straight from the world of artificial intelligence. It's great to be back with more news that's got me excited about the future.

First up, OpenAI has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise AI Coding Agents. This recognition is a big deal, as it highlights their innovation and ability to deploy at an enterprise scale with Codex. Essentially, this means OpenAI is helping companies automate coding tasks, making software development faster and more efficient. I've been following this space, and it's amazing to see how AI is changing the game for developers.

Another interesting development is AdventHealth's partnership with OpenAI. They're using ChatGPT for Healthcare to streamline workflows and reduce administrative burdens. This allows healthcare professionals to focus more on patient care, which is fantastic. It's amazing to see AI being used to make a positive impact in the medical field. The potential for AI to improve lives is huge, and it's great to see it being used in this way.

And, I came across another update that's worth mentioning. While I don't have all the details, it's clear that AI is being explored in many other industries, from finance to education. The possibilities are endless, and I'm excited to see where this technology takes us.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
